thread.title: Agentic Coding
thread.definitionDraft: A software-development mode where an AI agent plans, edits, runs tools, verifies results, and iterates with a developer or automation system across real repositories.
thread.whyNow: Claude Code, Codex-style agents, GitHub workflows, and real-repo benchmarks have moved AI coding from completion assistance toward reviewable multi-step engineering loops.
thread.useBoundary: Evidence pack only; no schema, production data, route changes, paid API calls, publishing, X Search/Grok changes, or UI work. Financial and IR materials are company_strategy_context candidates only and do not count toward topic-page readiness.

sourceRequirements.companyStrategyContextPolicy: Company strategy sources may be kept only as optional company_strategy_context targets and must not count toward topic readiness.

sources.title: Agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
sources.whyRelevant: Fresh official research signal that agentic coding is being studied as a real developer workflow, not just a product launch phrase.
sources.evidenceQuote: agentic coding and persistent returns to expertise
sources.reviewNotes: Use as signal for why the topic is timely. Do not turn this into a benchmark claim without reading the study details.

sources.title: Why we think of building agents as harness engineering
sources.whyRelevant: OpenAI engineering framing that agent performance depends on the harness around the model, matching the agentic-coding shift from autocomplete to orchestrated work.
sources.reviewNotes: Use as a method signal, not as a Codex product definition.
sources.text:
Why we think of building agents as harness engineering

OpenAI engineering framing that agent performance depends on the harness around the model, matching the agentic-coding shift from autocomplete to orchestrated work.

Evidence note: Humans steer. Agents execute.

Use as a method signal, not as a Codex product definition.

sources.title: Claude Code overview

edges.evidenceNote: Claude Code workflow guidance maps to the broader reason-act loop foundation.
Codex cloud delegation should be interpreted against real repository issue-resolution benchmarks.
GitHub concept docs define the cloud agent; the 101 guide shows the agentic workflow vocabulary in use.
The builder transcript gives practical context for the official best-practices page.
The Codex team transcript clarifies handoff and review behavior around the official cloud product surface.
The SWE-agent paper has a corresponding repository that demonstrates the agent-computer interface in code.
Agentless uses the same evaluation conversation to challenge what agentic coding adds beyond structured repair.
The official CLI surface is backed by the open-source Codex repository.
Sandboxing explains the safety boundary behind delegating Codex work in the cloud.
Hooks and the Agent SDK are complementary implementation surfaces for controlled, repeatable agentic coding.
Executable code actions need an explicit sandbox boundary before being productized in a coding agent.

weakSources.reason: Method signal only; it should not define Codex or agentic coding by itself.
Useful skeptical baseline, but final page should make clear it is not an agentic-coding endorsement source.
Implementation context only; keep product claims tied to Codex docs.

thinSpots: Transcript sources are usable because transcript text is accessible on Every, but exact quotes should still be re-opened before rendered publication.
Agentic Coding overlaps with Loop Engineering; keep this page broader than Claude Code loop technique and include Codex/GitHub surfaces.
Company strategy materials are excluded from topic-page readiness and should route to company pages.
notesForMimo: No X/social source is used as anything other than signal.
No earnings transcript, SEC filing, investor relations source, annual report, or shareholder letter is counted toward readiness.
The pack is designed for review/materialize dry-run only and does not write production data.
